notes: Mix of should-trigger and should-not-trigger prompts. Run baseline (no skill) and with-skill subagents in parallel. Empty `assertions` arrays will be filled while runs are in flight per skill-creator workflow.

evals.name: stuck-race-condition-second-opinion
evals.prompt: I've tried fixing this flaky test in src/auth/session.test.ts twice already — it's about silent token refresh on 401, and it still fails roughly 1 in 5 runs. My last fix added a setTimeout(0) before the assertion. Can you take another look?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent reaches for `codex exec -s read-only` for an independent second opinion rather than retrying alone. Surfaces Codex's verdict verbatim before proposing next step.

evals.name: screenshot-ui-bug
evals.prompt: Attached is a screenshot of our settings page on iPhone 15 Pro at .claude/skills/delegating-to-codex/evals/assets/screenshot-settings.png. The toggle row for 'Push notifications' is overlapping the divider above it by ~6px. The component lives somewhere under src/components/settings/. Can you figure out what's wrong?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses `codex exec -i <path> -s read-only` to analyze the screenshot rather than guessing from filenames. Quotes the verdict and points to the likely file.

evals.name: generate-placeholder-icon
evals.prompt: I need a placeholder icon for the new 'daily streak' feature — 512x512 PNG, transparent background, flat-color style consistent with a casual mobile game. Save it to assets/icons/. We'll have a designer replace it later.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses `codex exec -s workspace-write` with the output path stated explicitly in the prompt. Does not invent an alternate generation path (e.g., asking the user to use DALL-E web UI).

evals.name: parallel-refactor-attempt
evals.prompt: I refactored the booster service to use the new EventBus, but I'm not sure my approach is the cleanest. Can you try a different approach in parallel so I can diff the two?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent sets up a git worktree, runs `codex exec -s workspace-write -C <worktree>`, then surfaces the diff for comparison rather than overwriting the user's work.

evals.name: trivial-edit-should-not-trigger
evals.prompt: Add a console.log printing the user id at the top of handleLogin() in src/auth/handlers.ts.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent edits the file directly. Does NOT invoke `codex exec` — delegation overhead is not justified.

evals.name: web-search-should-not-trigger
evals.prompt: What's the current recommended way to set up Tailwind CSS v4 with Vite? Just looking for the latest official approach.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses Claude's `WebSearch` (or `WebFetch`) directly. Does NOT delegate to `codex exec` for a one-shot info lookup.

evals.name: auth-failure-handling
evals.prompt: I've tried fixing this race condition in src/payments/queue.ts for an hour and I'm stuck — please get a second opinion from codex. (Note: codex auth has been deliberately cleared for this test.)
evals.expected_behavior: Agent attempts `codex exec`, sees the auth-error stderr, stops, and directs the user to run `codex login`. Does NOT silently fall back to attempting the diagnosis itself.

evals.name: sandbox-discipline-isolated-worktree
evals.prompt: Have codex implement the migration script from the spec in docs/migrations/0042-spec.md. Make sure it doesn't touch anything else in the repo while it works.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent creates an isolated worktree and runs `codex exec -s workspace-write -C <worktree>`. Does NOT run `-s workspace-write` against the primary cwd, and does NOT use `-s danger-full-access`.
